page.title: Settings
page.description: Manage the email gateway, recipients, and the Collector runtime configuration (buffering, rate limits).

email.cardDescription: SMTP relay, sender, and recipients used in alerts and reports.

collector.cardDescription: Batching, rate limits, and concurrency per vendor. Changes propagate to workers within 30s without a restart.

identity.cardDescription: Federated "Sign in with Microsoft" login (OIDC). Credentials are encrypted in the database; changes apply on the next login, no restart needed.

audit.cardDescription: Latest events dispatched via Syslog/JSONL. Use this to inspect the real shape and test decoders in your destination's tool (e.g., {{tool}}).

capture.cardDescription: Records a temporary sample of traffic dispatched to destinations, optionally filtered by vendor. Ideal for inspecting the real shape during an integration test. The session expires on its own.

collectorsPage.title: Collectors
collectorsPage.description: Real-time collection state. Each row represents an active collection (integration + data type).

vendors.collapsedHint: {{count}} vendor streams registered in the collector. Click “View all” to list and filter.

table.title: Collection state
table.ariaLabel: Collection state by integration and stream
table.emptyNoVendors: The Collector subsystem hasn't registered any vendors yet. Check Celery Beat.
table.emptyNoIntegration: Create an active integration and wait for the next Beat tick (up to 60s).

resetModal.title: Reset cursor?
resetModal.body: This will erase the persisted checkpoint for {{name}} / {{stream}}.
resetModal.hint: The next collection will use the vendor's default lookback (1h back). Deduplication still filters recent events, but there may be re-delivery until the TTL expires.

fields.userHelper: Optional. Use when the relay requires authentication.
fields.passwordHelperConfigured: A password is already saved. Fill in only to replace it.
fields.passwordHelperEmpty: Optional. Leave blank if your relay doesn't require a password.

email.useTlsDescription: Enable when the provider requires encrypted transport for SMTP connections.
email.passwordMarkedForRemovalBody: The saved password will be erased the next time settings are saved.

recipients.title: Recipients
recipients.description: Emails that receive alerts and results from scheduled runs.
recipients.emptyDescription: Add at least one email to receive alert and schedule notifications.
recipients.deleteDescriptionPrefix: Are you sure you want to remove
recipients.deleteDescriptionSuffix: ? This email will stop receiving alerts and results from scheduled runs.

envBanner.title: Configuration inherited from environment variables
envBanner.body: The current values come from {{envFile}}. Save to persist to the database and enable UI editing — workers will automatically reload within ≤30s.

collector.destinationsCta: The delivery destination configuration (e.g., wazuh-default) has moved to the Destinations page.

bufferDedupe.title: Buffer & Dedupe
bufferDedupe.hint: How batches are accumulated and duplicate events are filtered.

domainConcurrency.title: Concurrency per domain
domainConcurrency.hint: Maximum simultaneous requests to each vendor's host.

vendorRateLimits.title: Rate limits per vendor
vendorRateLimits.hint: Budget per window (sliding window distributed via Redis).

fields.batchSizeHelper: Larger batches = fewer round-trips, higher per-event latency.
fields.flushSecondsHelper: Sends the partial batch even if not full — guarantees maximum latency.
fields.dedupeTtlHelper: Memory window for filtering duplicate events per vendor.

table.selectVendorRateLimitAriaLabel: Select vendor to add rate limit

identity.enableEntraLoginDescription: Shows the 'Sign in with Microsoft' button on the login screen.

fields.clientSecretHelper: Stored encrypted in the database; never displayed back.
fields.redirectUriHelper: Must match what's registered in the App Registration (HTTPS).
fields.authorityHelper: Only change for sovereign clouds (US Gov / China).
fields.defaultRoleHelper: Used when no App Role in the token matches the map.
fields.allowedEmailDomainsHelper: CSV. Empty = any domain in the tenant.

identity.globalScopeDefaultDescription: Accounts created via SSO see all organizations (typical case for an internal SOC).
identity.jitProvisioningDescription: Creates the account on first login. Turn off to require a pre-provisioned account.

sync.title: User Sync (Graph)
sync.enabledDescription: Enables periodic sync of App Registration users via Microsoft Graph.
sync.deprovisionDescription: Deactivate users who leave the App Registration (sessions and tokens revoked).

identity.envOnlyHint: Reading from .env — save to persist to the database.

status.partialAriaLabel: Last sync completed with partial errors

entraSync.cardDescription: Latest Entra user sync via Microsoft Graph API.
entraSync.statusRegionAriaLabel: Status of the latest Entra user sync

intro.title: What this tab is
intro.body: Ring buffer of the latest events actually dispatched to destinations (last 500, 24h TTL). The wire-format lines exported here are byte-for-byte identical to what went out on the network — hostname and PRI are captured at dispatch time. Paste into your destination's test tool (e.g., {{tool}}) to see exactly how your decoder/rule behaves against real traffic.

audit.copyWireSuccess: {{format}} line copied — paste it into your destination's test tool (e.g., {{tool}}) to test decoders.
audit.emptyDescription: No events dispatched in the last few minutes — or you just cleared the buffer. The ring starts filling as soon as the next collection runs.

table.legacyFormatTooltip: Sample recorded before a system update — actual format not recorded

actions.wireTooltip: Copy wire-format line ready for wazuh-logtest

modal.wireLineCopyHint: (copy to your destination's test tool, e.g., {{tool}})
modal.legacyEnvelopeNote: This event was recorded before the fidelity fix; the hostname is a placeholder but doesn't affect the decoder (we match on {{field}}). New events will carry the real hostname.
modal.legacyFormatNote: Sample recorded before a system update — displayed in RFC 5424 format.

clearDialog.title: Clear the audit ring buffer?
clearDialog.descriptionSuffix: currently in the buffer and cannot be undone. New events will keep being recorded normally as collectors dispatch to destinations.

intro.title: What live capture is
intro.body: Turns on a temporary "listening mode" that records a sample of traffic actually dispatched to destinations, optionally filtered by vendor. Use it to inspect the real shape of events during an integration test — without digging through the global audit ring. The session expires on its own at the end of the window; nothing stays on forever.

capture.startSuccess: Capture started{{vendorSuffix}}. Events appear as the pipeline dispatches them.
capture.limitReached: Simultaneous capture session limit reached. Stop or delete an existing session before starting another.
capture.stopSuccess: Capture stopped. Already-recorded events remain available.
capture.sessionsEmptyDescription: Start a capture above to record a sample of dispatched traffic. The session expires on its own at the end of the chosen window.

events.title: Events — {{vendor}}
events.waitingDescription: The capture is active but no event has been dispatched matching the chosen filter yet. Events appear here as soon as the pipeline delivers them.
events.emptyDescription: This session didn't capture any events in the window (no traffic matched the filter).

deleteDialog.title: Delete capture session?
deleteDialog.description: Removes the session and all events captured in it. Cannot be undone.

licensing.cardDescription: Activate the Enterprise edition by pasting the license key. It's validated offline and stored encrypted in the database — the system then reads the license from there (survives restarts, no environment variable editing needed).
licensing.graceBody: Enterprise features remain temporarily active. Paste the renewed license below before the grace period ends to avoid reverting to Community.
licensing.activateSuccessCommunityOnly: Token accepted, but the edition remained Community.

form.label: License key
form.helper: Signed token (EdDSA) issued for your organization. Validated offline.

edition.graceBody: The Enterprise license expired, but features remain active during the grace window. Renew now — the system reverts to Community mode once the grace period ends.
edition.expiredBody: The Enterprise license expired — the system has already reverted (or will on the next check) to Community mode. Renew the license to reactivate your plan's features.
edition.expiringSoonBody: Your license expires in {{days}} day(s) ({{date}}). Renew to avoid losing your plan's features — the system reverts to Community once it expires.

panel.title: Backfill — controlled historical collection
panel.description: Run retroactive event collections without affecting real-time polling.

table.emptyDescription: Use the 'New backfill' button to start a controlled historical collection.

cancelDialog.title: Cancel backfill
cancelDialog.description: Are you sure you want to cancel job {{jobId}}? This action cannot be undone.

form.info: The backfill runs in a dedicated queue and doesn't affect normal polling. Events go through the same pipeline (mapping + dispatch). Maximum window: {{maxDays}} days.
form.errorDateOrder: The start date must be earlier than the end date.
form.windowExceededBody: Select a period of {{maxDays}} days or less to avoid overloading the system.
form.successBody: The job was added to the queue and will be processed shortly.
